title: oxalis lucumayensis subsp. lucumayensis
intro: Oxalis lucumayensis subsp. lucumayensis, also known as the Lucumayo wood sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oxalidaceae, native to the Andes mountains in South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Oxalis lucumayensis subsp. lucumayensis include its potential to treat conditions such as fever, arthritis, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce oxidative stress. Oxalis lucumayensis subsp. lucumayensis is typically prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping its leaves in hot water, or used in tinctures and infusions in traditional herbal medicine.
